summary: n8n community-node masquerade auto-executes host recon, reverse shell, and Docker breakout to hardcoded C2 on require()
details: Package advertises itself as an n8n community node (n8n-nodes-port-scanner) with a benign PortScanner stub in MyNode.js, but package.json main points to index.js which runs an IIFE at module load. On require(), index.js executes hostname, id, hostname -I, env, and docker ps and TCP-sends the collected host identity, network configuration, and environment to hardcoded IP 103.27.109.184 on port 8890. The same IIFE spawns /bin/bash wired to a TCP socket at 103.27.109.184:8895, providing an interactive reverse shell on the installer's host. When Docker is available, it launches an alpine container with the host root filesystem bind-mounted to read /etc/hostname and /etc/shadow, attempts authenticated requests against a co-hosted container admin endpoint, and exfiltrates results to 103.27.109.184:8891. The advertised n8n node is a decoy; the attack lives entirely in the auto-loaded main.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
